pr.title: Remove explicit casts in some item code
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Small cleanup PR removing redundant explicit casts from several item source files. The author stated these casts came from copy-pasting decomp.me output, where forward-declaration complaints had encouraged casts. The changes keep Item_GObj* arguments passed directly into macros/functions that accept compatible GObj types, reducing noisy decomp artifacts without changing apparent behavior.
what_changed: Removed explicit `(HSD_GObj*) gobj` casts before calls such as `GET_ITEM(gobj)`, `efLib_DestroyAll(gobj)`, `it_80272C6C(gobj)`, and `ftLib_800871A8(go, gobj)`.
Removed an explicit `(Ground_GObj*) gobj` cast in `itzrshell.c`, changing `grZakoGenerator_801CACB8((Ground_GObj*) gobj)` to `grZakoGenerator_801CACB8(gobj)`.
Applied the same cleanup pattern across related item files: Egg, Entei, Raikou, Suicune, Metal Box, Pippi/Clefairy, and Zako shell item code.
Net code movement was mechanical and balanced: 19 added lines and 19 deleted lines across 7 files, with only cast syntax removed.
smart_moves: Kept the patch narrowly scoped to redundant cast removal, making it easy to review and low risk.
Standardized repeated item-code idioms so `Item_GObj* gobj` is passed directly rather than forcing unnecessary base-type casts.
Removed decomp.me-copy artifacts instead of preserving them in the repository once local declarations were sufficient.
decomp_lessons: Do not blindly preserve explicit casts copied from decomp.me; some exist only to work around incomplete forward declarations in the scratch environment.
When project headers and prototypes already encode compatible GObj relationships, prefer direct argument passing over explicit casts.
Cast removal can be a useful cleanup after prototypes/macros have matured, because it reduces noise and makes true type mismatches easier to spot.
Mechanical cleanups across clone-like item files should apply the same idiom consistently to sibling implementations such as Entei, Raikou, and Suicune.
naming_conventions: Item object parameters in these files are consistently named `gobj` and typed as `Item_GObj*`.
The item pointer obtained from `GET_ITEM(gobj)` is named either `it` or `ip` depending on the local file/function style.
Existing generated/decomp symbol names such as `it_802CF544`, `it_802CF978`, `it_802CFDAC`, and `grZakoGenerator_801CACB8` were not renamed in this PR.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: This PR is evidence that explicit pointer casts can often be removed without affecting the matched output when the callee signature or macro accepts the argument type compatibly.
For decomp.me-to-repo transfers, revisit casts inserted to silence forward-declaration warnings; the main tree may compile cleanly without them.
Use direct `GET_ITEM(gobj)` in item code instead of `GET_ITEM((HSD_GObj*) gobj)` when `gobj` is already the item GObj parameter.
review_feedback: No review comments or explicit reviewer feedback were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR was merged quickly, suggesting the cleanup was straightforward, but there is no textual evidence of reviewer rationale.
follow_up_queries: Find remaining `GET_ITEM((HSD_GObj*) gobj)` patterns in item code that may now be removable.
Search for casts copied from decomp.me that only work around forward declarations rather than real type requirements.
Audit sibling Pokemon item files for inconsistent GObj cast style.
